name: RABBIT-FEATURE-SCOPE-BUG-1
title: SKILL.md mixes shell syntax with Claude tool pseudo-code, causing agents to shell-exec Agent(prompt: ...)
description:
The rabbit-feature-scope SKILL.md Usage section shows a code block with two kinds of content: real shell code (PROMPT=$(...)) and Claude tool pseudo-code (# Dispatch Agent(prompt: PROMPT)). An agent reading this can misinterpret the comment as shell code to eval.

Observed failure: a previous session ran the resolve-scope.py command via Bash, then tried to call Agent(prompt: "$PROMPT") as a shell command. In tcsh, Agent(prompt: <text>) triggers glob expansion and fails with: (eval):2: no matches found: Agent(prompt: ...).

Root cause: the SKILL.md Usage block uses a bash code fence but mixes in Claude tool invocation syntax without clearly distinguishing the two. An agent following the block literally will attempt to eval Agent() from the shell, which is always wrong.

Fix: restructure the Usage section to separate (1) the shell command that generates the prompt, and (2) the instruction to Claude to call Agent() as a tool — using prose or a clearly-labeled non-shell block for step 2.

history.note: Fixed by PR #101: SKILL.md Usage section now uses separate fences for shell command vs Agent() tool call, with explicit prose warning between them. Inv 9 + e2e test coverage.
